subject: UX & Research
title: The Economic Case for Accessibility
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Economic Case for Accessibility.

hook.text: 1.3 billion people have disabilities worldwide. That's the third-largest 'country' by population.

buildup.text: The disability market controls over $8 trillion in annual spending power. Businesses that exclude them lose.

discovery.text: The Click-Away Pound study: 71% of disabled customers leave sites that are hard to use. They spend elsewhere.

twist.text: Companies see accessibility as a cost. But inaccessible sites lose more revenue than accessible ones cost.

climax.text: Accessible sites have better SEO, broader reach, and lower legal risk. The ROI case is overwhelming.

punchline.text: 1.3 billion potential customers. Inaccessible sites lose them. The ROI is clear.

quiz.question: What did the Click-Away Pound study reveal?
options: 71% of disabled customers leave inaccessible websites and spend their money elsewhere
Accessible websites cost 10x more to build
Only government websites need to be accessible
